programme.title: Sex, Cyanide And A Doctor On The Run Case
programme.sub-title: Linking The Death To A Homicide
programme.desc: The jury listened to the recorded interview the defendant gave police before fleeing the country.

programme.title: Sex, Cyanide And A Doctor On The Run Case
programme.desc: Asphyxiation by cyanide was the final conclusion about Rosemarie Essa's death.

programme.title: Sex, Cyanide And A Doctor On The Run Case
programme.sub-title: Searching For The Source Of The Cyanide
programme.desc: The lead detective in Rosemarie Essa's murder helped process her husband's extradition order since he was accused of being her killer. Detective Gary McKee told jurors that as soon as her death was determined to be a homicide, Yazeed was a suspect but he had fled the U.S. to go to Lebanon. So, where did Yazeed Essa get the solid cyanide that police alleged he used to kill his wife? Investigators looked for a link between the defendant and the poison, but they found nothing.

programme.title: Sex, Cyanide And A Doctor On The Run Case
programme.desc: The lead detective testified that investigators had the murder weapon that killed Rosemarie Essa, cyanide-laced calcium pills. But he admitted to jurors under cross-examination that his team couldn't link them directly to the defendant, Yazeed Essa. Jurors then heard again the timeline of events in this case under the cross-examination of Detective McKee.

programme.title: Sex, Cyanide And A Doctor On The Run Case
programme.desc: The Defense accused the lead detective in the case of making mistakes and leaving things out during the investigation into Rosemarie Essa's death. Jurors listened to details about the police's investigation into the defendant's affairs that went on while he was married to Rosemarie. Police initially had two of Yazeed's mistresses as potential suspects. Throughout this trial, the Defense even hinted to jurors that one of the women may be the real killer.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.desc: This special episode examines previous cases to learn about the investigative techniques used by the FBI.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1996, a group of political extremists robbed a bank in Washington using homemade bombs and military precision to escape. The FBI learned that the robbers might be preparing for a small-scale war with law enforcement.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The FBI discovered that the criminals had a hidden agenda beyond their personal wealth.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1993, an elderly man was found suffocated in his home with his checkbook, credit cards and vehicle stolen.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A pair of convicted felons escaped the custody of a local deputy and fled across the country in a wave of abductions and car thefts. When a foreign military officer disappeared along the trail, the FBI believed the pair were growing more violent.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A young woman went missing from her home on a bright spring morning. After several days of searching, agents suspected a paroled criminal who frequented her health club may have abducted her.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: After an Arkansas state trooper is shot and killed, officers catch the murderer and alert the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives when they find weapons and explosives in the man's vehicle.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In September 1997, a team of masked gunmen robbed an armored car facility in Los Angeles and made off with nearly $19 million, the largest cash heist in U.S. history. FBI agents began conducting surveillance on a former employee of the facility.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: In 2003, Kenneth Regan and William Horncy murdered a millionaire and his young family. The killers wiped out three generations of the Chohan family, including two young children.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Musician and special needs teacher Jane Longhurst vanished during her day off work after making a plan to go swimming with her friend's boyfriend. Five weeks later, Jane's partially decomposed body was found burning 25 miles from Jane's home.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Lonnie David Franklin Jr. is better known by the nickname 'Grim Sleeper.' He lured vulnerable women into his car before murdering and sexually assaulting them for more than two decades.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Billy Dunlop killed Julie Hogg in 1989 but was acquitted after two failed trials. However, due to a change in the UK law in 2005, Dunlop was convicted and sentenced to seventeen years in prison.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Billy Mansfield Jr. moved between Florida and Michigan, targeting vulnerable women and girls. Between 1975 and 1980, Mansfield Jr. murdered at least five victims, burying four of them at his family home in Spring Hill, Florida.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The chilling story of Mark Martin, who murdered three homeless women in his quest to become Nottingham's first serial killer.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Douglas Clark and Carol Bundy were a deadly duo who terrorised the streets of LA in the summer of 1980.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of one of the most prolific serial killers in Canadian history, dubbed The Pig Farmer Killer.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Years of unconnected home murders finally came together when cold-case detectives saw the pattern. They discovered that Emanuel, who moved far away, continued killing with the same familiar method.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Charlotte vanished before her birthday, and Johnny fed her family lies to hide the truth. Police slowly pieced together her final days, uncovering that Johnny killed her while the search for her remains continued.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A fugitive from the United States tackles the British Police and goes on the run.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Young Natalie Jarvis was killed by Adam Whelehan, whose cruel and cowardly plot was exposed by investigators.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: New Jersey native Richard Kuklinski ran a burglary ring, distributed pirated pornography and dealt in weapons and drugs. He also claimed to have murdered over 100 people while working as a hit man for New York and New Jersey's crime families.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Gloria, a devoted mother, tried to stop her son David's forbidden relationship. Her attempt to protect her family ended in tragedy as David's hidden violent streak erupted with devastating consequences.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: James 'Porky' O'Rourke's addiction to violence means he is one of the most feared men in the UK prison system.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Rocky Beamon lures a woman into the woods and drowns her. Once in prison, he brutally kills two fellow inmates - and vows to kill again.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Remorseless murderer Casey Pigge's main ambition is to be the ultimate serial killer. If unrestrained, he remains a deadly threat.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Imprisoned after a botched robbery that ends with the fatal shooting of an innocent man, Craig Wissink later kills a corrections officer with a fire extinguisher in a brutal attack.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Stephen Farrow committed two murders in early 2012 motivated by an obsessive hatred of Christianity, which he attributed to sexual abuse from a priest.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police investigate a missing college girl and discover her double life: attending school by day and working as a call-girl by night. Her body is found and investigators find her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A teen was killed by her parents in what they said was self-defense, but forensic evidence told otherwise.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When four-month-old Ryan died under suspicious circumstances, his mother was convicted of his murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1988, the body of a young woman was discovered in an Ohio river. Most of the evidence had been washed away, but the victim's 6-year-old son unwittingly told investigators all they needed to know - without saying a word. The clue was in his genes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When the body of a young woman is found under an isolated highway ramp, a unique fiber on the victim's clothing leads San Diego police straight to the most unlikely of killers.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1991, Maine resident Pearl Smith is missing after an argument with her husband Bill Bruns. But when an investigation turns up a blood trail that leads to the couple's basement, police are sure they'll find the victim's body.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a woman's bone marrow test reveals arsenic poisoning, investigators try to determine if it is due to groundwater contamination or something more sinister.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1985, 121 people in South Dakota and Minnesota were struck by a mysterious illness. There had been only one outbreak like it, and when it happened again no one could figure out why.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1996, 17-month-old Josh Hinson died in a fire which a federal agency ruled was intentionally set by Josh's mother Terri Strickland. Using the then-emerging resources of the Internet, Terri Strickland undertook her own investigation.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The disappearance of Philadelphia college athlete Aimee Willard is investigated after her car is found on the roadside. When her body is found, the police find unusual marks and DNA evidence that eventually lead to her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2002, a small community in upstate New York was devastated when Patty Wlasiuk died in a truck accident. But burdock seed pods found in her hair and on her clothes indicated that this was no accident - it was cold-blooded murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1992, Laura Houghteling disappeared from her Bethesda home and was never seen again. Laura's bedroom was searched and forensic science was used to direct them a to prime suspect.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A police officer accused of killing his estranged wife insists she committed suicide. Investigators say it was murder - that it was physically impossible for the woman to have shot herself.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A mother claims that an intruder murdered her two sons, but lacking a motive, police use blood-spatter analysis and behavioral profiling to reconstruct a different scenario.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A 19-year-old woman was found dead in her car, the scene had been staged to make murder appear to be suicide. During their investigation, police discovered the victim was involved in a love quadrangle, giving several people a motive to kill her.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A 1995 fire razes the mansion of a Kansas doctor, killing two of her three children; disturbing accounts from an ex-husband lead investigators to believe the doctor set the blaze herself.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The wife of a murdered horse rancher explains that the last time she saw her husband was when he was leaving the house to go jogging. Police become suspicious when forensic evidence eventually leads investigators back to the ranch.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A healthy young man dies mysteriously in his sleep, and toxicology tests reveal a lethal dose of lidocaine in his blood.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: On the night of May 22, 1992, Betty Wilson returned home and walked up the stairs to the bedroom. She discovered her husband lying in a pool of blood. Jack Wilson had obviously been murdered...but how? And by whom? Even the experts couldn't agree.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police are led on a wild goose chase when the wife of a prominent banker is kidnapped. An entomologist's unexpected discovery leads to a new suspect - and the realization that ordinary office equipment can leave extraordinary clues.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After 6-year-old Michelle Lee Dorr went missing, her father confessed to murdering her. But investigators soon poked holes in his story, leaving the case unsolved for 14 years.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When the decomposed body of a teenage girl is discovered, police have no clues to her identity. To solve the case, investigators turn to bug larvae found on the body.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The murders of an elderly couple leave police stumped until, two years later, they receive a major break.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman disappeared along a California highway in 1991. Three years later, the case was solved when a man's refrigerated truck was looked upon with suspicion.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman was reported missing after a fight with her husband. Police were suspicious of a suspect who reported a fire in his car. Two tiny drops of blood were found in the interior. A tiny clue inside the suspect's watchband helped solve the case.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman who survived a deadly childhood fire sends police a letter charging that her father killed her mother 30 years ago.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A mother of two vanishes after a shopping trip. Her body is discovered a month later. Witnesses say they saw the victim being forced into a car by an unknown person.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An infant was rushed to the emergency room with serious breathing problems. Within months, there were more than 30 cases. Doctors searched frantically for the cause and a cure.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Eleven-month-old Chad Shelton was admitted to the hospital with unexplained bleeding and later died. Investigators and scientists frantically searched for a cause when others from the same house fell ill.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The investigation into the kidnapping and rape of an 18-year-old Ohio woman is aided when the victim returns hours after the incident occurred. With her help, the police are able to track the assailant to his front door.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In the 1980s, a trio of unsolved murders occurred in Wichita Falls, Texas. Later, a fourth murder from the same time period provided the police with far more than they realized.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When Joann Katrinak is found dead in a corn field with her son, the obvious suspect is her husband. But insects found on the bodies reveal a vital clue which uncovers a twisted story.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A computer crash pushed a manufacturing company to the brink of collapse. Investigators must determine if the crash was the result of a sabotage.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Discovering that an assault suspect is left-handed is crucial to a police investigation dating back 16 years.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Time of death becomes pivotal after a pregnant woman is found murdered in her air-conditioned bedroom.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1995, when off-duty Maine State Trooper Vicky Gardner is attacked by Steven Fortin during a routine stop, it triggers a chain of events which jumpstarts a stalled murder case in New Jersey.
